project.title: Gas plasma combined with antibiotics for the eradication of methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus biofilm
project.projectDescription: Gas plasma treatment caused polymerization of the SaClpP proteins and loss of protein function due to the oxidative modifications induced by reactive oxygen species of the gas plasma.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Plasma-treated and untreated SaClpP (5 μg) were subjected to SDS-PAGE (15%) and visualized by staining with Coomassie brilliant blue R-250. The protein bands were excised, and the protein was digested in-gel with trypsin as described previously (Beranova-Giorgianni and Desiderio, 2000). The digested products were dissolved in 0.1% formic acid and purified with ZipTipC18 pipette tips (Millipore) according to the manufacturer’s procedure. The tryptic peptides were identified on an Orbitrap Fusion Tribrid mass spectrometer (ThermoFisher) coupled to an EASY-nLC 1000 UPLC system (ThermoFisher).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS data was processed using Mascot Daemon (v.2.5.1, 2014, Matrix Science, Boston, USA). Tandem mass spectra were searched against sequence database. Trypsin was specified as cleavage enzyme allowing up to 2 missing cleavages. For precursor ions, the mass error was set to 10 ppm, and for fragment ions, the mass error is set to 0.02 Da. Carbamidomethylation on Cys was specified as fixed modification and oxidation on Met was specified as variable modifications. False discovery rate thresholds for protein, peptide and modification site were specified at 1%. All the other parameters in Mascot were set to default values.
keywords: Gas plasma; biofilm; antibiotic; oxidative damage; methicillin-resistant staphylococcus aureus

references.referenceLine: Guo L, Yang L, Qi Y, Niyazi G, Huang L, Gou L, Wang Z, Zhang L, Liu D, Wang X, Chen H, Kong MG. Cold Atmospheric-Pressure Plasma Caused Protein Damage in Methicillin-Resistant Staphylococcus aureus Cells in Biofilms. Microorganisms. 2021 9(5)

Characteristics[Organism]: S. aureus ATCC33591, a methicillin-resistant strain
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Four replicates of biofilms were used for each treatment condition
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Fusion Tribrid mass spectrometer (ThermoFisher, Waltham, MA, USA)
Comment[Separation]: EASY-nLC 1000 UPLC system (ThermoFisher, Waltham, MA, USA)
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: purified with ZipTipC18 pipette tips (Millipore, Darmstadt, Germany) according to the manufacturer’s procedure.
